debug_points.description: No potholes visible in any of the 9 images. The road surface appears to be well-maintained asphalt with clear lane markings. The area shows residential properties with iron fencing and some commercial buildings. The pavement shows normal wear but no holes with missing asphalt or exposed base material.

The road surface appears to be in generally good condition. The asphalt is smooth and intact with proper lane markings visible. There is some water pooling visible in several images but no actual holes, missing asphalt, or exposed base material can be identified.

This is an urban street in Panama City with paved asphalt in generally good condition. The road shows normal wear patterns but no visible potholes or holes with missing asphalt. Various vehicles are parked along the street and the pavement appears intact.

The road surface appears to be in good condition with clean asphalt and clear lane markings visible. No potholes, holes, or missing asphalt sections are observed in any of the 9 images. The pavement shows normal wear but no structural damage.

The road surface at this intersection appears to be in generally good condition. There are visible patch repairs and some surface wear, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The intersection shows normal road markings, utility covers, and minor surface irregularities typical of urban streets.

This is a residential street corner in what appears to be Panama City. The road surface is asphalt in reasonable condition with no visible potholes. There are utility boxes on the sidewalk, landscaped areas with trimmed bushes, and a curved intersection visible.

This is a residential street in Panama City with parked vehicles on both sides. The road surface appears to be in generally good condition with intact asphalt. No potholes or holes with missing asphalt are visible in any of the 9 images.

This is a well-maintained residential street in what appears to be an upscale neighborhood in Panama City. The road surface is asphalt in good condition with visible lane markings. The surrounding area features brick paver driveways, landscaped gardens, and parked vehicles.

The road surface shows general wear, uneven patches, and wet conditions but no true potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The large gray-brown areas in multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts (blurry, textureless smears) that should be disregarded. The actual visible pavement shows rough texture and some depression areas but no holes with exposed base material or jagged broken edges.

No potholes visible in any of the 9 images. The road surface shows general wear and aging with some uneven patches, but no holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are present. The large gray-brown areas in the lower portions of most images are Street View stitching artifacts/blur, not road damage.

The road surface shows general wear and some debris/fallen leaves scattered on the pavement, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The stone/brick work visible appears to be decorative landscaping elements adjacent to the roadway, not road damage.

The road surface visible in these images appears to be in fair condition. The asphalt shows normal wear and some moisture/wet conditions but no actual holes with missing asphalt are visible. The gray/brown smears in the lower portions of multiple images are clearly Street View stitching artifacts from the close camera angle, not road damage.

No potholes visible in any of the 9 images. The road surface appears to be in generally good condition with intact asphalt. Views show a residential area with paved streets, sidewalks, and well-maintained infrastructure.

The road surface visible in these images is in good condition. The street shows well-maintained asphalt with clear lane markings, a landscaped median strip with small trees, and paved sidewalk areas. No potholes, holes with missing asphalt, or significant road damage are visible.

This is a residential street in Panama City with well-maintained asphalt pavement. The road surface shows normal wear but no visible potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges. The street has brick paver driveways, landscaping, and parked vehicles.

No potholes visible in any of the 9 images. The road surface shows general wear and aging but no holes with missing asphalt or exposed base material. The large gray/brown blurred areas in the lower portions of most images are Street View camera stitching artifacts, not water or road damage.

The road has uneven texture, some patches, and general wear consistent with aging pavement. The gray/brown blurred areas in the lower portions of several images are Street View stitching artifacts from the camera angle, not road damage or water pooling.

The road surface shows general wear and some uneven patching, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The large gray-brown areas in the lower portions of multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts (flat, textureless smears) rather than road damage or water pooling. The visible pavement shows rough texture and minor surface deterioration but no holes with exposed base material or broken edges.

The road surface appears to be in good condition with intact asphalt. The street is a residential/commercial area with parked vehicles. No potholes, holes, or missing asphalt visible in any of the 9 directional views.

The road surface appears to be in generally good condition. The asphalt shows normal wear patterns but no holes with missing material, exposed base layers, or broken edges are visible. There is some minor surface deterioration and leaf debris on the sidewalk areas, but the driving lanes appear intact.

The road surface shows general wear and aging but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The visible pavement shows normal urban road texture with some surface deterioration but no holes.

The road surface appears to be aged asphalt with some wear and minor surface irregularities, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The pavement shows typical urban wear patterns with some rough texture but maintains structural integrity.

The road surface appears to be aged asphalt in fair condition. No potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The pavement shows normal wear patterns but maintains structural integrity.

No potholes detected in any of the 9 images. The road surface shows typical wear patterns with some rough texture but no visible holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges. The large gray/brown areas at the bottom of most images are Street View stitching artifacts (featureless blurs), not road damage.

The asphalt shows normal wear and some minor surface irregularities but no holes with missing material, exposed base layers, or broken edges are visible. Some rocks/debris are visible on the grass verge but the roadway itself is intact.

The road surface shows general wear and aging with some minor surface deterioration, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The graffiti-covered wall runs along the right side of the street. The road appears to be unpaved or poorly maintained gravel/asphalt mix in sections.

This is a residential street intersection in what appears to be Panama City. The road surface is wet but intact asphalt in good condition. The large greenish-gray areas visible in multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts from the camera's downward/close angle, not actual road features or water pooling in holes.

The road appears to be flooded with significant standing water covering much of the street surface. However, this is pooled water on the roadway, not evidence of potholes. The visible pavement sections show intact asphalt without holes, broken edges, or exposed base material. The water appears to be collecting due to drainage issues or recent heavy rain, typical of this tropical climate area.

The road surface appears wet throughout all images, with water pooling visible in several views. However, no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth/broken edges can be confirmed. The wet asphalt shows normal wear patterns but no holes.

The road surface shows typical urban wear with minor surface irregularities but no visible potholes with missing asphalt. The large gray/brown areas in several images are clearly Street View stitching artifacts (textureless blurs without pavement detail), not water pooling or road damage. The actual visible road surfaces show intact asphalt with normal wear patterns.

No potholes visible in any of the 9 images. The large gray/brown blurred areas in the lower portions of most images are Street View camera stitching artifacts, not water pooling or road damage.

The road surface shows general wear and aging with some uneven asphalt texture, particularly visible in the northwest view. There are patch repairs visible and worn pavement, but no actual holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges that would constitute a true pothole.

There are some minor surface wear patterns and fallen leaves along the curb, but no holes with missing asphalt or exposed base material.

The road surface shows typical urban wear with some surface roughness and minor weathering, but no actual potholes are visible. The asphalt appears intact throughout all viewing angles. There are utility access covers and drainage infrastructure visible but no holes with missing asphalt.

No potholes visible in any of the 9 images. The paved areas include brick/herringbone patterned driveways and standard asphalt streets.

The large gray/green blurred areas in multiple images (North, Down, Northwest) are clearly Street View stitching artifacts, not water or road damage.

The actual asphalt road visible in images 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8 shows generally good condition with no visible potholes.

The visible pavement surfaces show normal wear but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, visible depth, or exposed base material.

The road surface is wet asphalt in fair condition. There is standing water on the road surface near the curb area, but no visible holes, missing asphalt, or exposed base material. The pavement appears intact with normal wear patterns.

The road surface appears to be in good condition with smooth dark asphalt and clear white lane markings. No potholes, holes, or missing asphalt sections are visible in any of the 9 images. The area shows well-maintained urban infrastructure with proper drainage grates visible in images 4-5.
